back_translation: And Samuel said to Saul, 'The LORD sent me to anoint you as king over His people, over Israel. Now therefore listen to the voice of the words of the LORD.'
Thus says the LORD of Hosts: I will punish what Amalek did to Israel, how he set himself against him in the way when he came up from Egypt.

Now go and strike Amalek, and devote to destruction all that is his — as cherem — and do not spare him. Put to death from man to woman, from child to nursing infant, from ox to sheep, from camel to donkey.'
And Saul summoned the people and numbered them at Telaim — two hundred thousand foot soldiers and ten thousand men of Judah.
And Saul came to the city of Amalek and lay in wait in the valley.

And Saul said to the Kenite, 'Go, depart, go down from the midst of the Amalekites, lest I sweep you away with him. For you showed steadfast-love to all the sons of Israel when they came up from Egypt.' So the Kenite departed from the midst of Amalek.
And Saul struck Amalek from Havilah as far as Shur, which is east of Egypt.
And he captured Agag king of Amalek alive, and all the people he devoted to destruction with the edge of the sword.

And Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep and the oxen and the fat calves and the lambs, and all that was good, and they were not willing to devote them to destruction; but everything despised and worthless, that they devoted to destruction.
And the word of the LORD came to Samuel, saying,
'I am sorry that I have made Saul king, for he has turned back from following me and has not carried out my words.' And Samuel was distressed and cried out to the LORD all that night.
And Samuel rose early to meet Saul in the morning, and it was told to Samuel, 'Saul has come to Carmel, and behold, he has set up a monument for himself, and has turned and gone down to Gilgal.'
And Samuel came to Saul, and Saul said to him, 'May the LORD bless you. I have carried out the word of the LORD.'
And Samuel said, 'Then what is this sound of sheep in my ears, and the sound of the cattle that I hear?'
And Saul said, 'They brought them from the Amalekites — for the people spared the best of the sheep and oxen, in order to sacrifice to the LORD your God. But the rest we devoted to destruction.'
And Samuel said to Saul, 'Stop, and let me tell you what the LORD spoke to me this night.' And he said to him, 'Speak.'
And Samuel said, 'Even though you were small in your own eyes, are you not head of the tribes of Israel? And the LORD anointed you king over Israel.
And the LORD sent you on a way, and said, Go and devote to destruction the sinful Amalek, and fight against him until you have wiped them out.
And why did you not obey the voice of the LORD? You rushed upon the plunder and did evil in the eyes of the LORD.'
And Saul said to Samuel, 'I have obeyed the voice of the LORD, and I went on the way that the LORD sent me, and I brought Agag king of Amalek, and Amalek I devoted to destruction.
And the people took from the plunder sheep and oxen — the best of the cherem-thing — to sacrifice to the LORD your God at Gilgal.'
And Samuel said, 'Does the LORD delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices as in obedience to the voice of the LORD? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, to listen than the fat of rams.
For rebellion is like the sin of divination, and arrogance is like the wickedness of idols. Because you have rejected the word of the LORD, He has rejected you from being king.'
And Saul said to Samuel, 'I have sinned, for I have transgressed the mouth of the LORD and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.
And now, please forgive my sin and return with me, so that I may worship the LORD.'
And Samuel said to Saul, 'I will not return with you, for you have rejected the word of the LORD, and the LORD has rejected you from being king over Israel.'
And as Samuel turned to go, he grabbed the hem of his robe, and it tore.
And Samuel said to him, 'The LORD has torn the kingdom of Israel from you today, and has given it to your neighbor who is better than you.
And the Glory of Israel does not lie and does not change his mind, for He is not a man, that He should change his mind.'
And Saul said, 'I have sinned. Now please honor me before the elders of my people and before Israel, and return with me, so that I may worship the LORD your God.'
And Samuel returned after Saul, and Saul worshiped the LORD.
And Samuel said, 'Bring to me Agag king of the Amalekites.' And Agag came to him cheerfully. And Agag said, 'Surely the bitterness of death has passed.'
And Samuel said, 'As your sword has made women childless, so your mother shall be childless among women.' And Samuel hacked Agag to pieces before the LORD at Gilgal.
And Samuel went to Ramah, and Saul went up to his house at Gibeah of Saul.
And Samuel did not see Saul again until the day of his death, for Samuel mourned for Saul. And the LORD was sorry that He had made Saul king over Israel.
